info.title: Babson Library Scheduler — Admin Assistant API
info.description:
Admin-facing API for the Babson Library student worker scheduling engine.

This spec is scoped for import into IBM Orchestrate as a custom extension.
It exposes only librarian / scheduler-admin operations: running preflight
checks, generating schedules, inspecting violations, reviewing student hour
summaries, and exporting the schedule to Excel.

Availability *collection* endpoints are excluded — the input source has not
been confirmed yet and will be added in a future extension update.

servers.description: Production — replace with actual host before deploying to IBM

tags.name: Shifts
tags.description: Generate shift instances for a week

tags.name: Schedules
tags.description: Schedule generation, retrieval, and assignment inspection

tags.name: Violations
tags.description: Constraint violations from the most recent schedule run

tags.name: Export
tags.description: Download the schedule as a formatted Excel workbook

get.description: Returns a simple liveness response. IBM Orchestrate can use this as a connectivity probe before running any admin action.
Returns the full roster of active student workers with their hour constraints and seniority dates. Useful for confirming who will be included in the next schedule run.

post.summary: Generate shift instances for a week
post.description: Slices the library's operating hours into shift blocks and writes them to the database for the given week. Must be run before generating a schedule. Idempotent by default — set `force: true` to wipe and regenerate.

422.description: Invalid week date or generation error

post.description: Analyses the current state of students, shift instances, and availability submissions for a given week. Identifies hard blockers (e.g. zero availability for a shift) and soft warnings (e.g. a student below minimum hours) before a schedule is generated. Saves a snapshot that can be retrieved later via GET.

200.description: Preflight report with feasibility verdict and findings list

422.description: Invalid week or missing shift data

get.summary: Retrieve the most recent preflight report for a week
get.description: Fetches the latest saved preflight snapshot for the given week. Run `POST /api/v1/diagnostics/preflight` first to generate one.

parameters.name: week_start_date
parameters.description: Monday of the target week in YYYY-MM-DD format (e.g. 2026-05-04)

200.description: Most recent preflight snapshot for the week

404.description: No preflight snapshot found for this week

post.summary: Generate a new schedule for a week
post.description:
Runs the CP-SAT constraint solver to produce an optimised assignment of student workers to shifts. The solver respects student availability levels, min/max weekly hour limits, seniority order, hard-shift priority, no-overlap, and maximum consecutive-hours rules.

Returns a schedule run record including fill rate and violation counts. If any **hard violations** are present the schedule will have `status: violated` and should not be published automatically.

200.description: Schedule run record with summary statistics

409.description: A run with locked assignments already exists for this week. Pass `force_regenerate: true` to override.

422.description: Validation error or missing prerequisite data

get.description: Returns all schedule runs in reverse chronological order. Useful for finding the `run_id` of the most recent draft or published schedule.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by run status: draft, published, or violated

get.summary: Get full schedule details for a run
get.description: Returns the complete schedule: run metadata, all shift assignments (with student names and preference levels used), and per-student hour summaries.

200.description: Full schedule with assignments and student summaries

get.summary: List shift-by-shift assignments for a run
get.description: Returns every shift assignment for the run — one record per shift slot — with the assigned student (or null if unfilled), the preference level used, and any override notes.

get.summary: Get per-student hour summaries for a run
get.description: Returns each student's total assigned hours, shift count, and whether they meet their minimum/maximum hour constraints. Useful for answering 'Which students are below minimum hours?'

get.summary: List all violations for a schedule run
get.description:
Returns every constraint violation detected by the postflight validator — both hard violations (block publishing) and soft violations (warnings). Violations are grouped by severity.

Common violation types:
- `UNFILLED_SHIFT` — no eligible student was available (hard)
- `COVERAGE_GAP` — a time window has no coverage (hard)
- `CONSECUTIVE_HOURS_EXCEEDED` — student assigned more than 6 consecutive hours (hard)
- `OVERLAPPING_ASSIGNMENT` — student assigned to two overlapping shifts (hard)
- `STUDENT_UNDER_MINIMUM` — student below their weekly minimum hours (soft)
- `PREFERENCE_VIOLATED` — student scheduled for a shift they marked cannot_work (soft)

200.description: All violations for the run (hard + soft)

get.description:
Returns only hard violations — the subset that must be resolved before a schedule can be published. **IBM Orchestrate branch condition:** an empty list means the schedule is safe to publish automatically; a non-empty list means human review is required.

This is the recommended endpoint to use as a decision gate in an automated workflow.

200.description: Hard violations only — empty array means schedule is publishable

get.summary: Download the schedule as a formatted Excel workbook
get.description:
Streams a multi-sheet Excel workbook (.xlsx) for the given schedule run. Sheets included:
1. **Dashboard** — key metrics and summary statistics
2. **Schedule** — shift-by-shift assignments with student names
3. **Student Summary** — per-student hours and constraint status
4. **Violations** — all violations grouped by severity
5. **Technical Details** — raw fields for admin reference

Hard shifts are highlighted in orange; unfilled shifts in red; under/over-hours students in red.

id.description: Student UUID — use this as `student_id` in other calls

seniority_date.description: Earlier date = higher seniority = scheduling priority

min_hours.description: Minimum hours the student must be scheduled per week

max_hours.description: Maximum hours the student may be scheduled per week

GenerateShiftsRequest.description: Request to generate shift instances for a week

slots_per_window.description: Number of concurrent desk positions per time window. Default is 1 (one student per shift). Increase only when a specific window genuinely requires more than one worker simultaneously.

force.description: Delete existing instances and regenerate from scratch

generated.description: Number of shift instance rows written

PreflightRequest.description: Request to run a preflight feasibility check

check_type.description: Type of check that produced this finding

severity.description: hard = blocks scheduling; warning = review needed; info = informational

recommended_action.description: What the admin should do to resolve this finding

PreflightReport.description: Preflight feasibility report for a week

is_feasible.description: False if any hard findings exist — scheduling will likely fail or produce violated results

hard_findings_count.description: Number of blocking issues that must be resolved

GenerateScheduleRequest.description: Request to run the CP-SAT scheduling solver

solver_time_limit_seconds.description: Maximum time allowed for the solver before it returns the best solution found so far

force_regenerate.description: Overwrite an existing draft. Blocked with 409 if any assignments are locked.

ScheduleRun.description: Summary record for a schedule generation run

id.description: run_id — use this in all subsequent schedule/violations/export calls

status.description: draft = generated but not reviewed; violated = hard violations exist; published = approved

solve_time_ms.description: Wall-clock time the solver took in milliseconds

objective_score.description: Solver objective value — higher is better

ShiftInfo.description: Shift instance details embedded in an assignment or violation

is_hard_shift.description: True for early-morning (07:30–09:00) and late-night (23:00–02:00) shifts

slot_index.description: 0 or 1 — index within a shared coverage window

StudentInfo.description: Minimal student reference embedded in an assignment or violation

Assignment.description: A single shift assignment in the schedule

preference_level_used.description: The availability level that justified this assignment

reason_codes.description: Solver explanation codes, e.g. SENIORITY_PRIORITY, UNDER_TARGET

is_locked.description: Locked assignments are preserved across re-generate calls

StudentSummary.description: Per-student hours summary for a schedule run

preferred_shifts.description: Shifts assigned where student had marked 'preferred'

available_shifts.description: Shifts assigned where student had marked 'available'

constraint_status.description: ok = within min/max bounds; under_minimum = soft violation

ScheduleFull.description: Complete schedule response including all assignments and student summaries

Violation.description: A constraint violation detected by the postflight validator

severity.description: hard = must resolve before publishing; soft = warning only

description.description: Plain-English description of the constraint that was violated
description.example: Sofia Nguyen is assigned 8.0 consecutive hours on Tuesday, exceeding the 6-hour maximum.
